title: 10 best herbal creams for gerd

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peppermint oil helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to GERD symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation.
Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can help alleviate GERD symptoms like chest pain and discomfort.

Soothing and coating property: The menthol in peppermint oil creates a protective barrier that soothes and coats the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing relief from acid reflux and heartburn.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with GERD because of its soothing and calming properties. The menthol in peppermint creams can help relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, peppermint's natural cooling sensation can help to numb the discomfort and pain associated with GERD, providing an almost instant feeling of relief and comfort, making it an effective treatment option for those suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has a relaxing effect on the muscles surrounding the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), thereby reducing symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn associated with Gerd.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate Gerd symptoms.
Menthone: Menthone has a mild relaxant effect on the smooth muscles of the LES, allowing stomach acid to flow more freely and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and Gerd symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils, particularly menthol and menthone, help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of GERD.
Stems: The essential oils present in the stems also have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate GERD symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation associated with GERD.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1 cup of fresh leaves of mentha x piperita in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Combine 1 cup of the plant extract with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before whipping it into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD) symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux.

Soften and protect mucous membranes: Licorice root's mucilages can coat and soothe irritated tissues, protecting them from further damage and reducing discomfort associated with GERD, while also helping to prevent further inflammation.
Antacid properties: The glycyrrhizin in herbal licorice creams has natural antacid properties, which help neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with GERD because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, licorice root has natural antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter and prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, providing much-needed relief for those suffering from GERD symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams help with GERD by forming a protective barrier in the stomach, reducing inflammation and preventing acid reflux.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux, providing relief from GERD symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the esophagus from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the risk of GERD complications and symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are widely used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate GERD symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the esophagus from acid reflux.
Barks: Barks are also utilized due to their glycosides content, which can help calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 1000ml of water for 30 minutes to create the base.
Filter the mixture and discard the solids then add 10g of sodium lauryl sulfate and mix well.
Heat the mixture at 70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes then add 10g of cetearyl alcohol and 10g of glycerin.
Mix the ingredients until they are fully incorporated then add 10g of stearic acid and 5g of vitamin e oil.
Transfer the mixture to a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: A compound found in Ceylon cinnamon, cinnamaldehyde has natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties that help reduce inflammation and neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.

Antioxidants: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams often contain high amounts of antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and healing existing damage.

warming and soothing properties: The natural warming and soothing properties of Ceylon cinnamon help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, improving digestion and reducing symptoms like bloating, gas, and discomfort associated with GERD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, creams helps with GERD because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. By applying a Ceylon cinnamon cream topically, it can help soothe and protect the esophageal lining, reducing the risk of acid damage and discomfort associated with GERD. This natural remedy offers a promising approach to managing GERD symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde, a major phenolic constituent in Cinnamomum verum, helps alleviate GERD symptoms by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, and also exhibiting antioxidant properties that protect against acid reflux.

Eugenol: Eugenol, a phenolic compound in Cinnamomum verum, exerts anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help mitigate GERD symptoms, including reducing the risk of acid reflux and alleviating esophageal inflammation.

Linalool: Linalool, a terpene constituent in Cinnamomum verum, has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and acid reflux, and promoting relaxation to alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help reduce acid reflux and alleviate Gerd symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cinnamomum verum are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of cinnamomum verum bark in 1 liter of water for 2 hours to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth to separate the bark from the liquid and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Combine the strained infusion with 100 grams of shea butter and 50 grams of lanolin in the melted wax mixture.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes until it thickens and cools to form a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative properties help to reduce bloating, gas, and discomfort in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
Antacid properties: The antacid compounds in fennel cream help neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from heartburn and indigestion associated with GERD.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can contribute to GERD symptoms such as discomfort and pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The volatile oils present in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, fennel's soothing properties may help calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate digestive issues, making it a promising alternative for those seeking relief from GERD symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: A terpene found in fennel, which helps relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing reflux and alleviating GERD symptoms.
Anethole: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux associated with GERD.
Saponins: A type of glycoside found in fennel, which may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are commonly used to make creams due to their high carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves are another key part used in creams for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate GERD symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare are used in creams due to their antispasmodic and carminative properties, which can help relax the digestive system and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried foeniculum vulgare at the peak of its fragrance to ensure maximum potency.
Infuse the dried herb in 1l of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the solids.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with 80% of a moisturizing cream base to create a smooth consistency.
Store the foeniculum vulgare cream in a cool dark place for up to 6 months to retain its therapeutic properties.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and discomfort.

Digestive enzymes and stimulants: Herbal ginger creams often contain digestive enzymes and stimulants like peppermint oil or chamomile, which can help stimulate digestion, relax the stomach muscles, and reduce symptoms of indigestion and nausea associated with GERD.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger has high antioxidant activity, which can help protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, potentially reducing inflammation and improving overall digestive health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with GERD because of its anti-inflammatory properties and natural digestive enzymes. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, ginger has been found to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, thereby providing relief from heartburn and discomfort associated with GERD. Regular use of ginger cream may help manage symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These are a type of phenolic compound that help with GERD by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter, thereby preventing acid reflux.

Shogaols: These are another type of phenolic compound found in ginger that help with GERD by inhibiting the production of gastric acid and improving digestion, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Terpenoids (specifically, gingerol-3-terpenoid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help with GERD by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux, such as heartburn and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of compounds that help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter and reduce acid reflux.
Barks: Barks are sometimes used to make creams for GERD as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Leaves are occasionally used due to their ability to help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of GERD through their antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 ounces of dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 cup of distilled water with 1/2 cup of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Whip 8 ounces of a neutral base such as beeswax with 16 ounces of the carrier oil mixture using an electric mixer.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm cream helps relax the muscles in the esophagus, reducing spasms and cramps associated with acid reflux and GERD symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream can help reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, which is common in individuals with GERD, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with GERD because it contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that soothes and calms the digestive tract. This anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux. By relaxing the muscles in the stomach and reducing symptoms of acid reflux, Melissa officinalis creams provides relief from GERD symptoms, promoting a more comfortable and peaceful digestive experience. Its calming effects also help to reduce stress and anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, thereby alleviating GERD symptoms.
Linalool: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter and reduce acid reflux.
Geranial: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the esophageal lining, thereby alleviating GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which provide anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties.
Stems: Stems of Melissa officinalis are sometimes used due to their lower concentration of essential oils, but they still contribute to the overall relaxing and calming effects of the creams.

(Optional) Roots: Although the roots are not as commonly used as the leaves and stems, they can be used in some herbal remedies for their potential anti-inflammatory properties, but their effectiveness in GERD creams is less documented.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested melissa leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours.
Steep 10 grams of dried melissa leaves in 100ml of carrier oil at 100f for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and essential oil in a double boiler at 180f for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from Gerd symptoms like heartburn and acid reflux.

Soothing and protective properties: Aloe vera's soothing and protective properties create a barrier on the skin and mucous membranes, shielding them from irritation and damage caused by stomach acids and enzymes, thereby reducing discomfort and pain associated with Gerd.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in herbal aloe creams help neutralize free radicals that can damage the esophageal lining and contribute to chronic inflammation, which is a hallmark of Gerd; antioxidants also promote healthy tissue repair and regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with GERD because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which work to reduce inflammation and neutralize stomach acid. This helps to alleviate heartburn and acid reflux symptoms associated with GERD. Additionally, aloe vera creams can help to protect the esophageal lining from further irritation, promoting a faster healing process and providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosides help alleviate GERD symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating gut motility, thereby preventing acid reflux.
Flavonoids: Specifically, flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin in Aloe vera possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the esophageal mucosa and reduce acid reflux.

Polysaccharides: Aloe vera's polysaccharides, such as acemannan, possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, alleviate GERD symptoms, and promote healing of the esophageal mucosa.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them a common ingredient in creams for GERD due to their ability to calm and protect the esophagus and stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 4-6 aloe vera leaves with a sharp knife and remove the outer green skin for use in the recipe.
Blend 1 cup of the aloe vera pulp with 1 tablespoon of distilled water to create a smooth consistency.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat.
Combine the blended aloe vera mixture with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a heat-proof bowl.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal lime creams containing anti-inflammatory herbs like ginger, turmeric, or marshmallow root can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms.

Antacid and acid-neutralizing properties: Herbal lime creams with ingredients like baking soda, calcium carbonate, or magnesium hydroxide can help neutralize excess stomach acid and provide quick relief from heartburn and acid reflux.

Soothing and protective mucilages: Herbal lime creams containing soothing herbs like slippery elm, aloe vera, or licorice root can help protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing discomfort and inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia x europaea, also known as lime, creams helps with GERD because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The lime flower's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, Tilia x europaea creams may help relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. This can provide relief for individuals experiencing heartburn, regurgitation, and other GERD symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
Terpenoids: Certain terpenoids, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing acid reflux and alleviating GERD symptoms.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the esophagus from oxidative stress caused by acid reflux, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Flowers: They have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
Buds: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect the digestive tract and alleviate GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia x europaea flowers on a warm sunny day in the summer months.
Dry the flowers in a cool well-ventilated area for at least 7 days using paper bags.
Steep 100 grams of dried flowers in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and press the solids to extract as much oil as possible.
Mix the extracted oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of distilled water to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, stomach lining, and digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and chest pain.

Antioxidant property: Brahmi's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with GERD.

Gastric protective property: Brahmi's gastric protective compounds help soothe and heal the stomach lining, reducing acid production and preventing further irritation from digestive enzymes, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri, also known as brahmi, creams helps with GERD because it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm the digestive tract. The herb has been traditionally used to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy digestion, Bacopa monnieri creams may provide relief from GERD symptoms, such as burning sensations and discomfort, allowing for a more comfortable and peaceful digestive experience. Its potential benefits make it a popular natural remedy.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of saponin glycosides found in Bacopa monnieri. They help with GERD by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut-brain axis, which can alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux.

Bacosterols: Bacosterols are a type of triterpenoid saponin found in Bacopa monnieri. They help with GERD by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the esophagus, which can prevent damage to the esophageal mucosa and alleviate symptoms.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: Phenylethanoid glycosides, particularly brahmoside and isobrahmoside, are found in Bacopa monnieri. They help with GERD by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter and reducing the frequency of acid reflux episodes, which can alleviate symptoms and prevent complications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bacosides, which are the key compounds responsible for the medicinal properties of Bacopa monnieri.
Roots: They contain a high concentration of bacosides and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate GERD symptoms.
Stems: They also contain bacosides and other compounds that may help soothe and protect the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of dried bacopa monnieri root powder.
Combine the powder with 100 grams of beeswax pellets and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 120-140 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of lavender essential oil to the mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Bile production stimulation: Dandelion's bitter compounds stimulate the liver to produce more bile, which can help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms of GERD by improving digestion and reducing acid production.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage associated with GERD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and inulin, work to calm the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. This helps to alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux, providing quick relief from the discomfort and pain associated with GERD. By promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing inflammation, Taraxacum officinale creams offer a natural and effective solution for managing GERD symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of GERD by soothing the digestive tract and reducing gut irritation.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpene saponin that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate GERD symptoms by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the esophageal lining from acid damage and reduce the severity of GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of GERD.
Roots: They are used due to their potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots in late summer or early fall for their medicinal properties.
Combine the roots with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until the beeswax melts.
Add 200 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it reaches a temperature of 40 degrees celsius.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 30 degrees celsius before adding 10 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it set for two hours before applying to the skin as a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with GERD because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the esophagus and stomach lining. The curcumin present in turmeric has natural antacid properties that neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, turmeric's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract helps alleviate symptoms like heartburn and regurgitation, providing long-lasting relief from GERD discomfort. As a result, applying topical turmeric creams to the chest area can bring rapid relief from GERD symptoms.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, reduces inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn.
Gastric mucosal protective effects: Turmeric extracts have been shown to protect the mucous membrane of the stomach from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing the risk of ulcers and erosion.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in turmeric creams help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, inflammation, and tissue damage in the esophagus and stomach, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, a type of underground stem, contains high amounts of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate GERD symptoms.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, making them effective against inflammation, oxidative stress, and acid reflux associated with GERD.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried turmeric powder into carrier oil by mixing 20 grams of turmeric powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and allowing it to steep for 48 hours.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the curcuma longa extract from the excess oil.
Combine the strained curcuma longa extract with beeswax pellets in a double boiler, heating until the wax melts and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer or whisk, ensuring a smooth texture is achieved after approximately 10 minutes.
Fill sterilized containers with the herbal curcuma longa cream, allowing it to cool completely and solidify at room temperature before applying to skin for gerd relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with GERD because they contain soothing and calming properties that ease digestive discomfort. The anti-inflammatory compounds in beach rose help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can occur when stomach acid flows back up and irritates the tissue. Additionally, the natural astringent properties of beach rose cream help to tighten the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up and alleviating symptoms of heartburn and indigestion associated with GERD.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosehip cream contains antioxidants and fatty acids that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which is a common symptom of GERD.

Softer tissue properties: The vitamin C and alpha-hydroxy acid present in rosehip cream can help break down scar tissue and improve skin elasticity, which may help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and chest pain associated with GERD.

Gastroprotective properties: Rose extracts have been shown to possess gastroprotective effects, which means they can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux and reduce inflammation in the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, providing relief from GERD symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant have been traditionally used to treat stomach ulcers and heartburn, making them a valuable component in medicinal creams for GERD.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosa x rugosa flowers and combine them with sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking daily, then strain through cheesecloth.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and coconut oil, melting the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy, adding vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel as desired.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and label, storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with GERD because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as caffeic acid and scopoletin, help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms like heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, stinging nettle has a natural antacid effect, helping to neutralize stomach acid and prevent further irritation. By providing quick relief from discomfort and pain, stinging nettle creams offer a natural alternative for managing GERD symptoms without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and chest pain.

Antacid properties: Nettle leaves have natural antacid properties that help to neutralize stomach acid and soothe digestive issues associated with GERD, providing quick relief from heartburn and indigestion.

Soothing demulcent action: The mucilage present in stinging nettle creams forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the esophagus, soothing and calming irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux, allowing for easier swallowing and reducing symptoms of GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing heartburn and reducing inflammation.
Fruits: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to neutralize acidity and reduce symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried nettle leaves and combine 20 grams with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the nettle's bioactive compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 20ml of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl set over simmering water.
Whisk the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has reached a smooth, creamy consistency suitable for application topically within 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Khat creams helps with Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD) because they provide a natural, soothing relief to the esophagus and stomach lining. The herbs in these creams work to calm inflammation, reduce acid production, and relax the muscles that can contribute to GERD symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation. Additionally, khat creams may also help to strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Licorice root: Anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of GERD.
Slippery elm: Mucilages in slippery elm help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
Marshmallow root: Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help calm acid reflux and heartburn symptoms by protecting the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and protect the digestive tract.
Rhyzomes: Contain alkaloids that can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing acid reflux symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with Gerd because of its soothing and calming properties, which can provide relief from the symptoms of acid reflux disease. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, thus alleviating heartburn and regurgitation. Additionally, chamomile's natural antioxidants help to protect the esophageal lining from further damage caused by stomach acid, promoting a healthy digestive system and reducing the frequency and severity of Gerd episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of GERD.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's natural soothing and calming effects on the digestive system can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), reducing acid reflux and heartburn symptoms associated with GERD.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with GERD.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol, which calms the esophagus and reduces irritation caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Combine the powdered chamomile with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 4 hours under room temperature conditions.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and transfer to a double boiler for heating.
Gently heat the infused oil mixture over low heat for 20 minutes or until the temperature reaches 60°c to 70°c stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat allow it to cool slightly then whip with an electric mixer for 2 minutes incorporating 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with GERD because it contains angelica archangelica, a natural ingredient that soothes and calms the digestive system. The cream reduces inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing gastric acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. This natural remedy provides relief for individuals suffering from GERD without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Angelica's antispasmodic compounds help relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing spasms and inflammation that can contribute to GERD symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal angelica creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with GERD.

Digestive stimulant property: Angelica's stimulating properties help improve digestion by increasing gastric acid production, which can aid in breaking down food and reducing symptoms of bloating, discomfort, and heartburn commonly associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Angelica roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with GERD.
Barks: The barks of the angelica plant contain compounds that exhibit carminative and anti-flatulent effects, reducing symptoms of heartburn and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried angelica archangelica root and leaf mixture from trusted sources for processing.
Combine 250ml of olive oil with the angelica archangelica mixture in a double boiler setup on low heat.
Infuse the mixture for 4 hours, stirring occasionally, to allow the herbal properties to dissolve into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Add 100g of beeswax pellets and 2% vitamin e oil to the infused oil, then heat and stir until fully emulsified.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with GERD because of its unique composition, which contains potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The creams' extracts from the maidenhair tree, also known as Ginkgo biloba, have been shown to soothe and calm the esophageal mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to protect and heal the damaged tissue, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn and discomfort. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for those suffering from GERD.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: These compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Gastric soothing agents: The creams' mucilages and saponins work to protect and soothe the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with GERD.

Antioxidant properties: The herbal extracts' antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate oxidative stress and damage in the esophagus and stomach, further contributing to GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with GERD, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted supplier or grow your own in a controlled environment.
Dry the fresh leaves using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 6 hours or air dry them for several days.
Blend 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a slow cooker at 50°c for 2-3 hours to infuse the oil with the herb's active compounds.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into sterilized glass jars, discarding the solids and storing the cream in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with Gerd because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that soothes and protects the esophageal mucous membranes. By reducing inflammation and irritation, milk thistle creams can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn, providing fast relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, further supporting digestive health and reducing the risk of complications associated with Gerd.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation and damage to the esophageal lining, alleviating symptoms of GERD.

Flavonoids: A type of polyphenol present in milk thistle, which has been shown to have protective effects on the mucous membranes of the esophagus, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux.

Saponins: A group of compounds found in milk thistle that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the production of stomach acid and relieve symptoms of GERD, such as heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the esophageal lining.
Seeds: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered herb with 200 grams of cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 2 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gerd are topical creams or ointments made from natural herbs and plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). These creams work by reducing inflammation and soothing the esophagus and stomach lining, thereby decreasing heartburn and discomfort. Examples include slippery elm and aloe vera creams, which have been shown to improve symptoms and quality of life for individuals suffering from GERD, allowing for better digestion and reduced medication reliance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to GERD that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea. These conditions often involve inflammation and irritation of the skin, which can be soothed by topical applications of herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula. Herbal creams may also help reduce itching, redness, and scaling associated with these conditions, providing natural relief for skin discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with GERD is a blend of slippery elm, aloe vera, and marshmallow root. Slippery elm soothes the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. Aloe vera calms the digestive system, while marshmallow root provides a protective barrier against stomach acid. Together, these creams can alleviate heartburn, discomfort, and digestive issues associated with GERD. Apply the combination topically to the chest or abdomen to experience relief from GERD symptoms.
